key_points: Secularism means that the state does not establish any official religion and treats all religions with equal respect
The Indian model of secularism allows the state to intervene in religious practices when they violate equality or justice
The Constitution guarantees the Right to Freedom of Religion under Fundamental Rights
Secularism promotes equality, religious tolerance and peaceful coexistence among different communities
The government ensures that no religion is discriminated against or given special privileges
Secularism strengthens democracy by protecting individual freedoms and preventing religious domination
examples: The Constitution allows citizens to freely practice, profess and propagate their religion
The government can ban practices such as untouchability even if they are justified through religion
Courts can take action if religious practices violate Fundamental Rights
People from different religions can live together and celebrate their festivals freely
formulas: Secularism → State neutrality + Equal respect for all religions
Religious freedom → Practice + Profession + Propagation of religion
Democratic harmony → Secularism + Equality + Fundamental Rights
exam_tips: Explain secularism with reference to the Indian Constitution
Mention the role of Fundamental Rights in protecting religious freedom
Explain how the state can intervene in religious matters to ensure equality
Give examples showing how secularism supports democracy and unity
